LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND SAN MATEO AND MARIN COUNTIES, INC.'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED AND THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION. WHILE MS. SAULSBERRY HAS SHOWN THAT THERE WAS PROCEDURAL UNCONSCIONABILITY IN THE MANNER IN WHICH SHE WAS ASKED TO SIGN THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, SHE HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE AGREEMENT IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. NOTHING IN THE AGREEMENT MAKES IT MORE LIKELY THAT MS. SAULSBERRY WILL BE REQUIRED TO PAY GOODWILL'S FEES THAN IF THE CASE WAS TRIED IN COURT. THE EXCLUSION PERMITTING EITHER PARTY TO SEEK INTERIM INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FROM A COURT DOES NOT RENDER THE AGREEMENT OTHER THAN BILATERAL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2016 Continued To Feb-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2016 Continued To Aug-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND SAN MATEO AND MARIN COUNTIES, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. While Ms. Saulsberry has shown that there was procedural unconscionability in the manner in which she was asked to sign the arbitration agreement, she has not shown that the agreement is substantively unconscionable. Nothing in the agreement makes it more likely that Ms. Saulsberry will be required to pay Goodwill's fees than if the case was tried in court. The exclusion permitting either party to seek interim injunctive relief from a court does not render the agreement other than bilateral. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)
